mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and supports multiple expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when policy is still easing from a restrictive stance.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS cuts are followed by negative or zero median 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered easing episodes do not outperform baseline in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and risk appetite, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when unemployment is accelerating rather than stabilizing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE acceleration episodes are followed by positive median 20-day SPX returns or if the sign is not negative in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A broad rebound in RSAFS indicates improving nominal final demand and revenue expectations, which supports equity earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if sustained RSAFS growth does not correspond to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears in rolling 3-year windows with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO with stable CPIAUCSL indicates supply recovery without a damaging inflation impulse, which supports margins and cyclical earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL inflation remains below 2.5% annualized over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent industrial recovery and subdued inflation do not produce higher 60-day SPX returns than either signal alone, or if joint episodes are not positive more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll losses combined with rising unemployment signal a demand shock severe enough to compress earnings expectations and elevate risk premia, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises in the same monthly release window
failure_condition: Reject if joint payroll loss and unemployment increase episodes are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns or if the combined signal is not stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts rebound after a collapse signals reacceleration in construction, durable-goods demand, and credit transmission, which lifts cyclical equity sectors and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 10% from its prior 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound episodes are not followed by above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal becomes negative in a majority of rebounds.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS indicates inflation pressure met by policy tightening; the resulting discount-rate and margin headwind produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if tightening-with-rising-inflation episodes do not produce negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint effect is not materially worse than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: During acute recession conditions, a rebound in GDPC1 after contraction signals that earnings expectations and credit stress are easing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE above 8% and GDPC1 turns positive versus its prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if GDP rebound episodes under high unemployment do not outperform baseline on 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign is not positive in at least 60% of such recessionary rebounds.
